name: Ceramic Risograph Dream
rationale: Renders the atelier as a soft two-color risograph print with the tactile warmth of handmade ceramics and 70s craft-zine design — the over-engineered cathedral bleeds ink and color-shifts into an impossible overlapping register mess, then collapses into a single clean clay bowl glowing at dead center, hitting the exact nostalgia-for-craft mood that dominates 2026 slow-making and cottagecore creator feeds.
preamble: Risograph print aesthetic rendered as a two-color spot-color illustration — dusty sage green and warm terracotta on cream paper stock; ink texture is grainy with deliberate misregistration halos (one color slightly offset from the other), paper grain visible throughout; linework is clean but slightly hand-drawn — no mechanical perfection, subtle wobble in long straight edges; shading done entirely through halftone dot-fields and flat color overlap, no gradients; the over-realized cathedral scene is dense and ink-dark, colors bleeding and overlapping into muddy register-mess shadows; the single honest object at composition center is isolated on clean cream with a tight terracotta halo and no halftone — pure, still, breathing; mood is quiet studio warmth meeting creative overwhelm; references: late-70s craft-zine illustration, Dinamo risograph editions, Kendra Allenby printmaking, Studio Ghibli prop design meets Etsy-core handmade aesthetic; square crop, centered composition, generous cream border.
